theme: Buy redundancy so the link never drops
title: Buy Redundancy So The Link Never Drops
strategies: trivial: spend nothing, every edge stays at its base failure probability (== the checker's own zero-upgrade baseline) -> ~0.1

greedy: fractional-knapsack over EDGES in isolation -- rank every (edge, next upgrade level) item by its own probability delta per unit cost and buy top-ranked affordable items in that fixed order, never looking at how much redundancy already backs an edge up

strong: decompose the network by 2-edge-connectivity (Tarjan bridges) into independent bridge/blob multiplicative factors, then do exact steepest-ascent hill climbing on the TRUE reliability (recomputing the real marginal delta of every candidate purchase every step) -- correctly discovers that value concentrates on lone bridge edges (which sit in every near-minimum cut of their group) while a redundant parallel fan is already self-insuring and further spend there is nearly wasted
invalid: push every edge's upgrade level to 99 (far past its cap) -> infeasible, scores 0
